summaryForNext: All 3 goals are wiring, not invention — the primitives exist and are tested. G1 (NOT MET): agent_governance_lint_routes(&[RouteConfig]) already lints any slice (config/types.rs:157) but nothing calls it with the DB-merged set; the merged Vec<RouteConfig> is built and thrown away in router.rs:128 at startup (main.rs:257) and reload (cache/mod.rs:398-406). HARD CONSTRAINT: the reload path has NO bail!/exit — a live process can only rebuild-or-retain, so reload-time governance must WARN or reject-route-and-retain-last-good, never terminate (unlike startup bail! at main.rs:180). G2 (NOT MET): CedarBundle::from_records already merges any &[PolicyRecord] (bundle.rs:91) and the sugar already compiles to Vec<PolicyRecord> with collision-safe ids (sugar.rs:95); the gap is that sugar records aren't carried into the DB engine build/reload paths, so a refuse-start guard (main.rs:306-315) stands in. reload_from_database (cache/mod.rs:372, admin/mod.rs:727) is DB-only and would DROP sugar on every reload — the central fix is persisting sugar on AuthzEngine + concatenating on reload. Cedar forbid-wins is the precedence backstop; PolicyIds don't collide. G3 (NOT MET): the allow/deny->Cedar compiler exists server-side (sugar.rs:72) but is YAML-only — no admin route, no client fn, no UI (grep confirms zero matches). Policies page is raw-Cedar-only (web/src/pages/Policies.tsx); AgentIdentities.tsx (modal builder form + RQ hooks) and Routes.tsx (nested structured config) are the two UI templates; /policies CRUD + validate_policy 400-gate + reload_from_database is the backend pattern. Build order G1->G2->G3 (G3 should write into the merged set G2 establishes). Zero new deps. 5 open questions for Analyze: (1) G1 reload error model (WARN vs reject-and-retain; startup-strict-bail vs reload-can't-exit); (2) G1 surface merged route set vs recompute; (3) G2 sugar reload persistence (store on engine+concat vs write to authz_policies rows); (4) G2 sugar-permit-vs-DB-forbid precedence tests; (5) G3 UI write target (config sugar vs DB rows vs compile-endpoint) — depends on G2's overlay-vs-DB-row decision. Each change touches authz/policy/reload -> separated security review each. Federate any JWKS IdM (Ory reference), never an IdP; LLM-ops out of scope.
